entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop (Pennisetum glaucum) studied for GE interaction in semi-arid tropics

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The measure of grain production, affected by GE interactions in drought environments

entities.name: GENOTYPE-BY-ENVIRONMENT INTERACTION
entities.description: The interaction between genetic makeup and environmental conditions affecting trait expression

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A lack of water availability causing stress to crops

entities.name: WATER AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The amount of water available to the crop throughout the season

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The thermal environment, specifically maximum temperatures during grain filling

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The broad geographic region characterized by semi-arid tropical environments

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The specific country where the 24 environments were located

entities.name: METEOROLOGICAL ENVIRONMENTAL DESCRIPTIONS
entities.description: The use of simple meteorological data to describe environments and understand GE interactions

entities.name: ADAPTIVE PLANT TRAITS
entities.description: Traits that allow a plant to adapt to specific environmental stresses

entities.name: BREEDING PROGRAMS
entities.description: Systematic efforts to improve crop varieties through selection and crossing

relationships.description: Study focuses on GE interaction of pearl millet
GE interaction characterizes grain yields in drought environments
Water availability is a factor in GE interaction
Temperature is a factor in GE interaction
Heat stress is a major factor determining GE interaction
Pearl millet is grown in semi-arid tropics
The semi-arid tropical environments were in India
Used to provide understanding of GE interaction
Knowledge of GE interaction facilitates identification of adaptive traits
Knowledge of GE interaction enables rational choice of test locations for breeding programs
